MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or an objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court 3.1385. Judge: NEWTON LAM; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-25-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Nathan, Esq. 415-341-1144 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Hunter Sims III, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court shares his views on the Heskel case. Defendant argues. Plaintiff argues there is a triable issue of material fact, cites exhibits on papers, GC 835.2(b)(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument vacates the tentative ruling posted on 1/23/18 and rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied for reasons stated on the record. There is s triable issue on trivial defect and constructive notice. The triable issue on dangerous condition remains. The testimony As to Constructive notice that talks about 3-5 years remains. In addition, as to the obvious nature issue, there is a triable issue created by paragraphs 26 & 28 of the Fitz declaration. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Plaintiff to prepare MSJ order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312 that indentifies 26 & 28 as evidence that creates a TIF on constuctive notice.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. HUNTER W. SIMS III, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-4259 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted in its entirety without leave to amend and its request for monetary sanctions is denied. Plaintiff James Sollenberger's attempt to allege a general negligence claim against the City separate and apart from a premises liability claim based on dangerous condition of public property fails for two reasons. First, California law does not recognize such a claim. Second, while Government Code 840.2 authorizes a premises liability claim based on dangerous condition of public property against employees of a public entity, Mr. Sollenberger's complaint does not identify any public employees he contends is liable per section 840.2 nor does he allege any specific negligent or wrongful act of any employee. Mr. Sollenberger has not shown that there is any reasonable possibility that he will succeed in his efforts to allege a cognizable negligence claim against the City or any of its employees distinct from the claim he has already alleged against the City founded on CCP 835 and thus this motion is granted without leave to amend. The City's request for monetary sanctions is denied because the City did not comply with the requirements of CCP 128.7. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn ; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar as moot. On May 13, 2016, plaintiff filed a first amended complaint that moots the instant motion. The instant motion is directed to the original complaint and the amended complaint supersedes the original complaint. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131 (filing of amended complaint rendered calendared motion for summary judgment moot). There is no prejudice to defendant. There is no trial date and defendant can re-file the summary judgment motion against the operative complaint. The court further notes that defendant, as a public entity, will not need to pay a filing fee for the renewed motion.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Plaintiff General Negligence Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Paul Nathan of The Law Offices of Paul H. Nathan telephone number 415-341-1144 for Plaintiff James Sollenberger.
Hunter Sims of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4259 for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Plaintiff General Negligence Cause Of Action is granted as to the first cause of action with 20-days leave to amend to allow Counsel if he can do so in good faith allege a vicarious liability claim under the tort claims act. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Nathan of The Law Offices of Paul H. Nathan telephone number 415-341-1144 for Plaintiff James Sollenberger.
Hunter Sims of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4259 for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JAMES SOLLENBERGER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION on his first cause of action for general negligence is denied. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. The motion is based on the mistaken premise that plaintiff has stated a cognizable general claim against the City. Neither San Francisco Public Works Code 706 or Department of Public Works Order 177,525 is a permissible basis for a negligence claim against a public entity. Per the Torts Claims Act, "the term 'statute' does not include local ordinances or regulations." (Societa Per Azioni De Navigazione Italia v. City of Los Angeles (1982) 31 Cal.3d 446, 463.) A local guideline is also not a statute for the purposes of negligence per se. (Evidence Code 669.1.) Moreover, the motion is procedurally improper since it seeks the disposition of less than the entirety of a claim and is not confined to the issue of whether or not the City owes a legal duty to Mr. Sollenberger. (CCP 437c(f)(1)). Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
